rulings.Case Title: BAY RIDGE PROPERTIES LLC VS. AMERICAN BROKERS CONDUIT CORPORATION et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT And Or Motion For Summary Adjudication Against 1. Devon J. Bolla, As Trustee For The Broderick Street Trust, Dated August 19, 2013 And 2. Bill Olin , As Trustee For The Bill Olin 401-K Profit Sharing Plan
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for August 23, 2017 line 1. INTERVENOR WELLS FARGO, NATIONAL ASSOCIATION's MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT And Or Motion For Summary Adjudication Against 1. Devon J. Bolla, As Trustee For The Broderick Street Trust, Dated August 19, 2013 And 2. Bill Olin , As Trustee For The Bill Olin 401-K Profit Sharing Plan DENIED. Motion for summary adjudication of the causes of action for quiet title and equitable lien is DENIED. Triable issue of fact whether Broderick Street Trust and Olin are bona fide purchaser/encumbrancer. Facts 13, 16, 19, 22. Motion is denied as the cause of action for cancellation of instrument. No evidence or authority is cited for the proposition that these documents are dependent and cannot remain in the chain of title in the absence of 2013 Judgment. The issue remains whether Broderick took title free of 2007 DOT and whether Olin's DOT has priority over 2007 DOT, not whether these documents are improper and subject to cancellation. The Court finally notes that Wells Fargo is referring to itself as the "owner" of the Property and to the Property as "belonging" to Wells Fargo, however, the evidence provided by Wells Fargo only indicates Wells Fargo's claim as a beneficiary under the 2007 Deed of Trust. = (501/REQ)

rulings.Case Title: BAY RIDGE PROPERTIES LLC VS. AMERICAN BROKERS CONDUIT CORPORATION et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION On Its First Cause Of Action Against Bay Ridge Properties, Llc
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for August 23, 2017 line 1. INTERVENOR WELLS FARGO, NATIONAL ASSOCIATION's MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION On Its First Cause Of Action Against Bay Ridge Properties, LLC DENIED. No evidence or authority is provided by the moving party for the proposition that Grant Deed recorded August 7, 2013 transferring interest from Velasquez to Bay Ridge (encumbered or not) is "wild" or dependent on a void Judgment. No authority is cited for the proposition that Vlelasquez, owner and trustor, could not have transferred his interest in the property to a third party in the absence of a judgment. The Court additionally notes that the Grant Deed was executed on June 21, 2013 before the July 26, 2013 Judgment was entered. The Court finally notes that Wells Fargo is referring to itself as the "owner" of the Property and to the Property as "belonging" to Wells Fargo, however, the evidence provided by Wells Fargo only indicates Wells Fargo's claim as a beneficiary under the 2007 Deed of Trust = (501/REQ)

rulings.Case Title: KAZIM Z ACAR VS. JENNIFER E CRANE
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion For Reconsideration Of Order To Dismiss Acar'S Complaint And Memo Of P&A; Declaration Of Kazim Acar
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for August 23, 2017 line 2. PLAINTIFF KAZIM ACAR's Motion For Reconsideration Of Order To Dismiss Acar'S Complaint DENIED. Moving party fails to meet the requirements of CCP 1008. = (501/REQ)

rulings.Case Title: 29 SW, LLC VS. ONY HOMES, LLC ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Attorneys Fees
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for August 23, 2017 line 3. DEFENDANTs ONY HOMES, LLC, PREMIER ESTATES, INC., ANTON DANILOVICH's Motion For Attorneys Fees GRANTED. Defendant is entitled to recover only the attorney's fees incurred in making a motion to expunge lis pendens per CCP 405.38. Upon review of the invoices submitted in support of the motion the Court determines that Defendant is entitled to recover $9,928.5 limited to reasonably incurred in non-duplicative necessary fees in making a motion to expunge lis pendens. Plaintiff fails to establish substantial justification for recording the lis pendens (no evidence provided by Plaintiff re: probability of prevailing on the merits at any point in this action) or maintaining of lis pendens after the dismissal of the action. = (501/REQ)

rulings.Case Title: ALI KHOSTOVAN VS. PETER CURTIS HARTKA ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc Of Mtn For Order Entering Judgment And Enforcing Settlement
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for August 23, 2017 line 4. PLAINTIFF ALI KHOSTOVAN's Motion For Order Entering Judgment And Enforcing Settlement DENIED. Moving party failed to establish that it is entitled to enforce the settlement agreement while in breach in its own obligations under the Settlement Agreement regarding specified repairs. = (501/REQ)

rulings.Case Title: ANGEL RILEY VS. TIMOTHY BALLARD ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION / Notice Of Motion & Motion To Compel Further Response To Discovery And Request For Monetary Sanctions Against Pltf; Memorandum Of Points And Authorities
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for August 23, 2017 line 5. DEFENDANTs TIMOTHY BALLARD and MARY BALLARD's MOTION To Compel Further Response To Discovery And Request For Monetary Sanctions Against Pltf Hearing Required. Parties to appear at 9:00am to engage in good faith meet and confer efforts. Parties to report their progress at 9:30am. = (501/REQ)

rulings.Case Title: JIM JOHNSON VS. LISA BLACKWELL ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Defendant'S Motion To Vacate Default And Default Judgment; Or For Other Appropriate Relief: Declaration Of Defendant Debonne Blackwell, Declaration Of Defendant'S Counsel Philip Rapier; Proposed Demurrer To Complaint; Proposed Order.
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for August 23, 2017 line 6. DEFENDANT LISA BLACKWELL's Motion To Vacate Default And Default Judgment; Or For Other Appropriate Relief GRANTED. Defendant's request for discretionary relief under CCP 473(b) is granted on the condition that Defendant (1) pays Plaintiff's counsel $1,140 within 10 days of notice of entry of order; (2) file its proposed demurrer no later than August 23, 2017 by 4:00 p.m.; and (3) post $17,500 in daily damages (calculated as July 2017 to August 2017, plus estimated time before trial in October 2017 based on demurrer being the proposed response). = (501/REQ)
